145.reason: [If the character after '&' is not '&', this will access out-of-bounds when i is at the last index, causing an IndexOutOfBoundsException. Additionally, even if within bounds, it fails to handle the case where a single '&' appears at the end of file correctly—it should report error 'a' but may crash before that.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [If the character after '&' is not '&', this will access out-of-bounds when i is at the last index, causing an IndexOutOfBoundsException. Additionally, even if within bounds, it fails to handle the case where a single '&' appears at the end of file correctly—it should report error 'a' but may crash before that.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [If i is at the last character of content, content.charAt(i) will cause IndexOutOfBoundsException]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [If the '&' is the last character in the input, accessing content.charAt(i) will cause an IndexOutOfBoundsException, but more importantly, after incrementing i, if i is beyond the string length, the condition check fails and error handling is triggered incorrectly even when there's no next character. However, the real issue is that after detecting a single '&', the code reports an error but does not decrement i to reprocess the next character (which was skipped by i++), causing the next character to be skipped unintentionally.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [If i is at the last character of content, content.charAt(i) will cause IndexOutOfBoundsException]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [If the input ends right after a single '&', this access content.charAt(i) will cause an IndexOutOfBoundsException, but more importantly, even if within bounds, when '&' is at the end of file or followed by non-'&', it should report error 'a'. However, after incrementing i, if i >= content.length(), the condition content.charAt(i)=='&' will throw an exception instead of handling the error correctly.]

159.reason: [Same issue as above: if '|' is the last character in the input, content.charAt(i) will throw StringIndexOutOfBoundsException. The code does not check whether i is still within bounds before accessing content.charAt(i).]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue as above: if '|' is the last character in the input, content.charAt(i) will throw StringIndexOutOfBoundsException. The code does not check whether i is still within bounds before accessing content.charAt(i).]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [If i is at the last character of content, content.charAt(i) will cause IndexOutOfBoundsException]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue as with '&': after incrementing i, if the next character is not '|', an error is reported, but the loop index i is not adjusted back, causing the next character (which was read and found not to be '|') to be skipped entirely in subsequent processing. This leads to missed tokens or incorrect parsing.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [If i is at the last character of content, content.charAt(i) will cause IndexOutOfBoundsException]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue as above: if '|' is the last character in the input, i++ makes i == content.length(), and content.charAt(i) will throw StringIndexOutOfBoundsException instead of detecting the lexical error 'a'.]
